IES HOLDINGS, INC.

FORM 10-K

PART I

Item 1. Business

The company provides electrical contracting, communications, infrastructure, and industrial services across multiple end markets.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Demand for construction and infrastructure services may decline if customers delay capital projects or if labor availability tightens.

Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments

None.

PART II

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue increased due to project execution and backlog conversion. Gross margin changed with project mix and labor costs.

Item 7A. Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk

Market risk disclosure begins here.

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data

Consolidated financial statements begin here.

Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements

Note 1. Summary of Significant Accounting Policies

Revenue is recognized over time for many contracts based on progress toward completion.

Item 9. Changes in and Disagreements With Accountants on Accounting and Financial Disclosure

None.
